mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic of return difference is insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without stagflationary pressures, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term market momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of periods following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 12-month rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates combined with decelerating nonfarm payrolls indicate monetary tightening is successfully suppressing economic activity, raising recession probabilities and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive forward returns in > 55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in retail sales demonstrates resilient consumer spending power, boosting near-term corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where RSAFS exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive IC decays below 0.01 within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal expanding construction activity and rising household wealth effects, which historically precede broader economic expansion and generate positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive skew when HOUST trends upward, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in backtested signal portfolios.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in total nonfarm payrolls and an increase in the unemployment rate confirms a broad-based labor market deterioration, triggering defensive portfolio rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the joint condition fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02 compared to single-series models.
